evals.prompt: My product listing page renders thousands of products. It's static content that rarely changes. What rendering strategy should I use?
evals.expected_output: Use SSG (Static Site Generation): fetch with cache: 'force-cache'. Use generateStaticParams for dynamic product routes. For periodic updates (new products): use ISR with revalidate: 3600. This gives best performance (CDN cached) while keeping content reasonably fresh. Avoid SSR for static content.

evals.prompt: My dashboard page has a slow data fetch (3 seconds) that blocks the entire page from loading.
evals.expected_output: Use Streaming with Suspense: wrap slow components in <Suspense fallback={<Skeleton />}>. Create loading.tsx for route-level skeleton. Or use PPR (Partial Prerendering) with ppr: true in next.config.js — static shell renders immediately, dynamic regions stream in. Never root-await slow fetches in page.tsx.

evals.prompt: My page uses window.innerWidth in the initial render and shows a hydration error.
evals.expected_output: Violation: No typeof window in initial render. window.innerWidth is browser-only and causes hydration mismatch. Fix: use the mounted useEffect pattern — const [mounted, setMounted] = useState(false); useEffect(() => setMounted(true), []); if (!mounted) return null. Or use dynamic import with ssr: false for the component.

should_not_trigger: Configure a NestJS dependency injection module
Write a Go middleware for rate limiting
Set up Kubernetes horizontal pod autoscaling
